title: Ditching news hype for daily AI market structure at the US Open
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer building herculmarket.com, I realized that the hardest part of the trading day is filtering out the noise during the first 30 minutes of the US session. To stay objective, I’m now using my AI analyzer to generate a daily technical briefing on BTC, NVDA, and Gold exactly at 15:30 CET.

Why I’m moving toward this systematic approach:

Structural Clarity: The AI maps out liquidity sweeps and Order Blocks on the M15/H1 timeframes, ignoring the emotional chaos of the opening bell.

Verified Execution: This system helped me secure a 150 profit with a 2.75 RR on Gold recently by sticking to the data instead of the headlines.

Data Integrity: All performance metrics are logged in a PostgreSQL backend, calculating real-time XIRR and drawdown to ensure my edge is statistically sound.

Risk Hardcoding: The terminal forces me to validate my setup's math before I even consider clicking a button.

I’m opening a few more beta spots on herculmarket.com for those who want to follow these daily structure updates and track their own performance with engineering-level rigor.

See you at the next US Open. Are you guys trading the initial volatility or waiting for the structure to settle? 📈💻

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body: Mapping liquidity sweeps and order blocks with AI is fine, but it’s still just labeling price action after the fact. If it keeps you disciplined at the open, great—edge usually comes from execution and risk, not the briefing.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Spot on. Labels don't pay the bills—execution and risk management do.

We aren't claiming to have a "magic crystal ball" that replaces a trader's discipline. The goal of the **HerculMarket** morning briefing is precisely what you mentioned: discipline at the open**.** By mapping out liquidity sweeps and order blocks at 9:30 AM EST (15:30 CET), we provide a consistent framework so you aren't reactive when the volatility hits. It’s about having a professional-grade starting point for your own execution.

Think of it as a high-signal pre-game ritual for BTC, NVIDIA, and Gold. If it saves you 30 minutes of charting and keeps your head clear for the actual trade, it’s worth the €15/month.

The Beta ends this Monday, March 23—come see if the briefing sharpens your edge:[https://herculmarket.com/](https://herculmarket.com/)
